---
title: "Programming guide (双语对照)"
description: "Programming guide — 中英双语对照阅读"
keywords: ["双语对照", "中英对照", "bilingual", "翻译", "ROCm", "AMD", "GPU"]
source_url: https://rocm.docs.amd.com/en/latest/how-to/programming_guide.html
source_type: bilingual
original_lang: en
---

import BilingualViewer from "@site/src/components/BilingualViewer";

export const enContent = "<p><span class=\"fa-solid fa-angle-right\"></span></p>\n<ul>\n<li><a href=\"../index.html\" class=\"nav-link\" aria-label=\"Home\"></a></li>\n<li>Programming guide</li>\n</ul>\n<h1 id=\"programming-guide\">Programming guide</h1>\n<h1 id=\"programming-guide-1\">Programming guide<a\nhref=\"#programming-guide\" class=\"headerlink\"\ntitle=\"Link to this heading\">#</a></h1>\n<p><span class=\"sd-pr-2 article-info-date-svg\"><img\nsrc=\"data:image/svg+xml;base64,\"\nclass=\"sd-octicon sd-octicon-calendar\" /></span> 2026-01-23</p>\n<p><span class=\"sd-pr-2 article-info-read-time-svg\"><img\nsrc=\"data:image/svg+xml;base64,\"\nclass=\"sd-octicon sd-octicon-clock\" /></span> 3 min read time</p>\n<p>Applies to Linux and Windows</p>\n<p>ROCm provides a robust environment for heterogeneous programs running\non CPUs and AMD GPUs. ROCm supports various programming languages and\nframeworks to help developers access the power of AMD GPUs. The natively\nsupported programming languages are HIP (Heterogeneous-Compute Interface\nfor Portability) and OpenCL, but HIP bindings are available for Python\nand Fortran.</p>\n<p>HIP is an API based on C++ that provides a runtime and kernel\nlanguage for GPU programming and is the essential ROCm programming\nlanguage. HIP is also designed to be a marshalling language, allowing\ncode written for NVIDIA CUDA to be easily ported to run on AMD GPUs.\nDevelopers can use HIP to write kernels that execute on AMD GPUs while\nmaintaining compatibility with CUDA-based systems.</p>\n<p>OpenCL (Open Computing Language) is an open standard for\ncross-platform, parallel programming of diverse processors. ROCm\nsupports OpenCL for developers who want to use standard frameworks\nacross different hardware platforms, including CPUs, GPUs, and APUs. For\nmore information, see <a href=\"https://www.khronos.org/opencl/\"\nclass=\"reference external\">OpenCL</a>.</p>\n<p>Python bindings can be found at <a\nhref=\"https://github.com/ROCm/hip-python\"\nclass=\"github reference external\">ROCm/hip-python</a>. Python is popular\nin AI and machine learning applications due to available frameworks like\nTensorFlow and PyTorch.</p>\n<p>Fortran bindings can be found at <a\nhref=\"https://github.com/ROCm/hipfort\"\nclass=\"github reference external\">ROCm/hipfort</a>. It enables\nscientific, academic, and legacy applications, particularly those in\nhigh-performance computing, to run on AMD GPUs via HIP.</p>\n<p>For a complete description of the HIP programming language, see the\n<a href=\"https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/index.html\"\nclass=\"reference external\"\ntitle=\"(in HIP Documentation v7.2.53211)\"><span\nclass=\"xref std std-doc\">HIP programming guide</span></a>.</p>";

export const zhContent = "<p><span class=\"fa-solid fa-angle-right\"></span></p>\n<ul>\n<li><a href=\"../index.html\" class=\"nav-link\" aria-label=\"Home\"></a></li>\n<li>\u7f16\u7a0b\u6307\u5357</li>\n</ul>\n<h1 id=\"\u7f16\u7a0b\u6307\u5357\">\u7f16\u7a0b\u6307\u5357</h1>\n<h1 id=\"\u7f16\u7a0b\u6307\u5357-1\">\u7f16\u7a0b\u6307\u5357<a href=\"#programming-guide\"\nclass=\"headerlink\" title=\"Link to this heading\">#</a></h1>\n<p><span class=\"sd-pr-2 article-info-date-svg\"><img\nsrc=\"data:image/svg+xml;base64,\"\nclass=\"sd-octicon sd-octicon-calendar\" /></span> 2026-01-23</p>\n<p><span class=\"sd-pr-2 article-info-read-time-svg\"><img\nsrc=\"data:image/svg+xml;base64,\"\nclass=\"sd-octicon sd-octicon-clock\" /></span> 3 \u5206\u949f\u9605\u8bfb\u65f6\u95f4</p>\n<p>\u9002\u7528\u4e8e Linux \u548c Windows</p>\n<p>ROCm\uff08ROCm\uff08Radeon \u5f00\u653e\u8ba1\u7b97\u5e73\u53f0\uff09\uff09\u4e3a\u8fd0\u884c\u5728 CPU \u548c AMD GPU\n\u4e0a\u7684\u5f02\u6784\u7a0b\u5e8f\u63d0\u4f9b\u4e86\u4e00\u4e2a\u5f3a\u5927\u7684\u73af\u5883\u3002ROCm\uff08ROCm\uff08Radeon\n\u5f00\u653e\u8ba1\u7b97\u5e73\u53f0\uff09\uff09\u652f\u6301\u591a\u79cd\u7f16\u7a0b\u8bed\u8a00\u548c\u6846\u67b6\uff0c\u4ee5\u5e2e\u52a9\u5f00\u53d1\u8005\u5229\u7528 AMD GPU\n\u7684\u5f3a\u5927\u6027\u80fd\u3002\u539f\u751f\u652f\u6301\u7684\u7f16\u7a0b\u8bed\u8a00\u662f\nHIP\uff08HIP\uff08\u5f02\u6784\u63a5\u53e3\u53ef\u79fb\u690d\u6027\uff09\uff09(Heterogeneous-Compute Interface for\nPortability) \u548c OpenCL\uff0c\u4f46 HIP\uff08HIP\uff08\u5f02\u6784\u63a5\u53e3\u53ef\u79fb\u690d\u6027\uff09\uff09\u7ed1\u5b9a\u53ef\u7528\u4e8e\nPython \u548c Fortran\u3002</p>\n<p>HIP\uff08HIP\uff08\u5f02\u6784\u63a5\u53e3\u53ef\u79fb\u690d\u6027\uff09\uff09\u662f\u4e00\u4e2a\u57fa\u4e8e C++ \u7684 API\uff0c\u4e3a GPU\n\u7f16\u7a0b\u63d0\u4f9b\u8fd0\u884c\u65f6\u548c\u5185\u6838\u8bed\u8a00\uff0c\u5e76\u4e14\u662f ROCm\uff08ROCm\uff08Radeon\n\u5f00\u653e\u8ba1\u7b97\u5e73\u53f0\uff09\uff09\u7684\u57fa\u672c\u7f16\u7a0b\u8bed\u8a00\u3002HIP\uff08HIP\uff08\u5f02\u6784\u63a5\u53e3\u53ef\u79fb\u690d\u6027\uff09\uff09\u4e5f\u88ab\u8bbe\u8ba1\u4e3a\u4e00\u79cd\u8f6c\u6362\u8bed\u8a00\uff0c\u5141\u8bb8\u4e3a\nNVIDIA CUDA\uff08CUDA\uff08\u7edf\u4e00\u8ba1\u7b97\u8bbe\u5907\u67b6\u6784\uff09\uff09\u7f16\u5199\u7684\u4ee3\u7801\u5bb9\u6613\u5730\u79fb\u690d\u5230\u5728 AMD GPU\n\u4e0a\u8fd0\u884c\u3002\u5f00\u53d1\u8005\u53ef\u4ee5\u4f7f\u7528 HIP\uff08HIP\uff08\u5f02\u6784\u63a5\u53e3\u53ef\u79fb\u690d\u6027\uff09\uff09\u7f16\u5199\u5728 AMD GPU\n\u4e0a\u6267\u884c\u7684\u5185\u6838\uff0c\u540c\u65f6\u4fdd\u6301\u4e0e\u57fa\u4e8e\nCUDA\uff08CUDA\uff08\u7edf\u4e00\u8ba1\u7b97\u8bbe\u5907\u67b6\u6784\uff09\uff09\u7684\u7cfb\u7edf\u7684\u517c\u5bb9\u6027\u3002</p>\n<p>OpenCL (Open Computing Language) is an open standard for\ncross-platform, parallel programming of diverse processors. ROCm\nsupports OpenCL for developers who want to use standard frameworks\nacross different hardware platforms, including CPUs, GPUs, and APUs. For\nmore information, see <a href=\"https://www.khronos.org/opencl/\"\nclass=\"reference external\">OpenCL</a>.</p>\n<p>Python bindings can be found at <a\nhref=\"https://github.com/ROCm/hip-python\"\nclass=\"github reference external\">ROCm/hip-python</a>. Python is popular\nin AI and machine learning applications due to available frameworks like\nTensorFlow and PyTorch.</p>\n<p>Fortran bindings can be found at <a\nhref=\"https://github.com/ROCm/hipfort\"\nclass=\"github reference external\">ROCm/hipfort</a>. It enables\nscientific, academic, and legacy applications, particularly those in\nhigh-performance computing, to run on AMD GPUs via HIP.</p>\n<p>For a complete description of the HIP programming language, see the\n<a href=\"https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/index.html\"\nclass=\"reference external\"\ntitle=\"(in HIP Documentation v7.2.53211)\"><span\nclass=\"xref std std-doc\">HIP programming guide</span></a>.</p>";

<BilingualViewer enContent={enContent} zhContent={zhContent} sourceUrl="https://rocm.docs.amd.com/en/latest/how-to/programming_guide.html" />
